title: Maven Build & Dependency Management
overview: Understanding Apache Maven, the standard project management and build automation tool used in Java testing frameworks.
whyItMatters: Maven simplifies project setup by automatically managing dependencies (jars) and providing a standardized build lifecycle to compile, test, and package applications.
explanation: Maven uses a `pom.xml` (Project Object Model) file to manage project dependencies, plugins, and configurations. It fetches dependencies from the central repository. The Maven build lifecycle includes phases like `clean`, `compile`, `test`, `package`, and `install`. SDETs primarily use the `maven-compiler-plugin` (to compile Java code) and `maven-surefire-plugin` (to execute TestNG/JUnit tests).
keyPoints: POM.xml is the heart of a Maven project.
Dependencies are defined using GroupID, ArtifactID, and Version (GAV).
The default build lifecycle runs sequentially (running `mvn test` also runs validate and compile).
The Surefire plugin is responsible for running unit and integration tests.
codeExample:
<!-- pom.xml snippet for Selenium and TestNG -->
<dependencies>
    <dependency>
        <groupId>org.seleniumhq.selenium</groupId>
        <artifactId>selenium-java</artifactId>
        <version>4.15.0</version>
    </dependency>
    <dependency>
        <groupId>org.testng</groupId>
        <artifactId>testng</artifactId>
        <version>7.8.0</version>
        <scope>test</scope>
    </dependency>
</dependencies>
realWorldExample: Instead of manually downloading Selenium and TestNG JAR files and adding them to the build path, an SDET simply adds them to the pom.xml, and Maven downloads them automatically.
commonMistakes: Committing the `target/` directory to version control (it should be in `.gitignore`).
Having dependency conflicts (using mismatched versions of interconnected libraries).
Forgetting to specify the Surefire plugin, causing `mvn test` to not execute any tests.
bestPractices: Use Maven profiles (`<profiles>`) to manage different environments (dev, qa, staging).
Define versions in the `<properties>` block to maintain consistency across dependencies.
Regularly run `mvn clean` before building to ensure a fresh state.

interviewQuestions.q: What is the difference between `mvn clean` and `mvn test`?
interviewQuestions.a: `mvn clean` deletes the `target` directory (compiled output). `mvn test` executes the validate, compile, and test phases of the build lifecycle using the Surefire plugin.

interviewQuestions.q: How do you run a specific TestNG suite xml file from the command line using Maven?
interviewQuestions.a: Configure the maven-surefire-plugin in the pom.xml to accept a parameter, then run `mvn test -DsuiteXmlFile=testng.xml`.

revisionSummary: Maven = Dependency management (pom.xml) + Build lifecycle (clean, compile, test). Surefire plugin executes tests.
